subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 09
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering geography, nature, science, health, and technology for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is a reptile?
questions.explanation: Turtles are reptiles, which means they are cold-blooded, have scales, and lay eggs on land.
questions.hint: They have a hard shell on their back.

questions.q: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River in Africa is generally considered the longest river in the world.

questions.q: Which device do we use to see very tiny objects like bacteria?
questions.explanation: A microscope is an optical instrument used to view objects that are too small to be seen by the naked eye.
questions.hint: It makes small things look huge.

questions.q: Which part of the body controls your movements and thoughts?
questions.explanation: The brain is the command center of the human body.
questions.hint: It is protected by your skull.
questions.objective: Understand the function of major human body organs.

questions.q: How many planets are in our solar system?
questions.explanation: There are eight recognized planets in our solar system: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.
questions.hint: It is more than five but less than ten.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system.

questions.q: Which of these is a healthy habit to keep your body strong?
questions.explanation: Physical activity like running and playing helps keep your heart, muscles, and bones healthy.
questions.hint: It involves moving your whole body.

questions.q: What do we call a scientist who studies stars and space?
questions.explanation: An astronomer is a scientist who studies stars, planets, and the universe.

questions.q: Which fruit is known for being high in Vitamin C and used to make juice?
questions.explanation: Oranges are famous for their high Vitamin C content and are commonly juiced.
questions.hint: It has the same name as its color.

questions.q: Which landmark is located in Sydney, Australia?
questions.explanation: The Sydney Opera House is a multi-venue performing arts centre in Sydney, New South Wales, Australia.
questions.hint: It looks like large white sails.

questions.q: What gas makes up most of the air we breathe?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78 percent of Earth's atmosphere.
questions.hint: It is not oxygen, but it is in the air all around us.
questions.objective: Understand basic scientific facts about the atmosphere.
